name: Senegalese Chicken Yassa
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1/8 teaspoon minced fresh Habanero or other hot chile
One chicken (2 1/2 to 3 1/2 pounds), cut into serving pieces
1 Habanero or other hot chile, pricked with a fork
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large nonreactive bowl, prepare a marinade with the lemon juice, onions, salt, pepper, minced chile, and 1/4 cup peanut oil. Place the chicken pieces in the marinade, ensuring they are well covered, and allow them to marinate for at least 2 hours in the refrigerator.
2. Preheat the broiler. Remove the chicken pieces from the marinade, reserving it, and place them in a shallow roasting pan. Broil until lightly browned on both sides.
3. Remove the onions from the marinade. Cook them slowly in the remaining tablespoon oil in a flameproof 3-quart casserole or Dutch oven until tender and translucent.
4. Add the remaining marinade, pricked chile, pimiento-stuffed olives, carrots, mustard, and water to the pan. Stir well, then bring the yassa slowly to a boil.
5. Lower the heat and simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through. Serve hot over white rice.
Yield: 6 servings
